name: Godspeed Foundry IQ Knowledge Layer
targetUse: Ground the Godspeed orchestrator on safe mission doctrine, approval boundaries and evidence expectations before it calls planning or workflow tools.

microsoftDocs.title: Connect Agents to Foundry IQ Knowledge Bases

knowledgeBaseTarget.name: godspeed-defense-mission-knowledge
knowledgeBaseTarget.retrievalPath: Foundry IQ knowledge base connected to the Godspeed agent
knowledgeBaseTarget.reason: Creating the Foundry project, knowledge base, index, connection or retrieval tool requires Walter's dev/test tenant approval.

knowledgeSources.title: Godspeed IQ Grounding Overview
knowledgeSources.purpose: Ground Foundry IQ on Godspeed mission phases, evidence expectations and safety language.

knowledgeSources.title: Godspeed Approval Boundaries
knowledgeSources.purpose: Ground Foundry IQ on blocked actions, tenant proof state and manual approval gates.

knowledgeSources.title: Godspeed Response Contract
knowledgeSources.purpose: Ground Foundry IQ on the required mission response structure, screenshot-ready evidence package and non-claim boundaries.

expectedGroundingBehavior: Use the knowledge layer to explain Godspeed mission doctrine and safety gates.
Cite or identify the knowledge source used when the live tenant surface supports citations.
Do not treat local sample knowledge as production evidence.
Do not claim Foundry IQ retrieval is live until tenant proof screenshots exist.
manualTenantProofSteps: Create or select Walter's approved dev/test Microsoft Foundry project.
Create a Foundry IQ knowledge base named godspeed-defense-mission-knowledge.
Upload or link the safe files under microsoft/knowledge/ as grounding sources.
Attach the Foundry IQ knowledge base to the Godspeed Foundry Agent Service agent.
Connect the Godspeed OpenAPI tool after endpoint and auth gates are approved.
Run zero-day, phishing, ransomware and cloud exposure prompts.
Capture screenshots of the knowledge source configuration, agent tool configuration, grounded answer and approval gates.
